mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and triggers negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales signals robust consumer spending that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates weakening manufacturing demand, causing immediate negative equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in greater than 60 percent of late-cycle observations or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 3.5 percent constrain credit availability and increase borrowing costs for corporations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of high-rate periods or regression slope is positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and corporate revenues, yielding positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Combined signal fails to predict positive returns in greater than 50 percent of cases or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and consumer confidence, which directly translates into stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of high-start periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 115 during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that suppress equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal generates positive returns in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances or predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent in walk-forward tests.
